items.prompt: Which memory is counted as auxiliary space?

options.text: Additional working storage used to compute the result, excluding pre-existing input and separately reported output.
Every byte occupied by the input.
Every required returned value.
The source-code text length.

feedback.reason: Auxiliary space describes extra working state created for computation.
feedback.details: Input, output, and auxiliary storage should be classified by role.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.input_always: Input storage is normally reported separately.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.output_always_aux: Required result storage is output space.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.code_size: Algorithmic space analysis concerns runtime storage.

items.prompt: A function creates many temporary arrays one after another, but frees each before creating the next. What determines peak auxiliary space?

options.text: The largest amount simultaneously alive, not the sum of all allocations over time.
The total number of bytes ever allocated.
Only the final allocation.
Only the number of variable names.

feedback.reason: Space complexity is a peak-concurrency measure.
feedback.details: Sequential reuse can keep peak memory low even when cumulative allocation volume is large.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sum_all_allocations: Space complexity measures peak live memory.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.last_only: An earlier allocation may have been larger.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.variable_count: Containers can differ greatly in size.

items.prompt: An iterative algorithm uses ten scalar variables regardless of n. What is its auxiliary space?

options.text: O(n) because the variables process n inputs.
Impossible to classify without their names.

feedback.reason: A fixed number of fixed-size values does not scale with input size.
feedback.details: The time spent updating them is separate from storage size.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.linear: Storage count does not grow with n.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.ten_n: The variables are not duplicated per input.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.unknown: A fixed number of fixed-size scalars is sufficient information.

items.prompt: A recursive function has depth n and each frame stores O(1) local state. What stack space does recursion use?

options.text: O(n).
O(1) because each frame is constant-size.
O(n²) because recursion is nested.
O(log n).

feedback.reason: Peak stack memory equals active depth times per-frame storage.
feedback.details: n frames with constant state require O(n) space.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.constant: There are n simultaneously active frames.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.quadratic: Constant frame size multiplied by depth n is linear.

items.prompt: A hash map stores one entry per distinct input value, and there are d distinct values. What parameter gives the tight auxiliary-space expression?

options.text: O(d).
O(n) only.
O(1) because each lookup is expected constant-time.
O(log d).

feedback.reason: Storage follows the number of retained keys.
feedback.details: When every input value is distinct, d can equal n; otherwise O(d) is more precise.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.always_n: O(n) is a worst-case bound, but d is the tighter direct parameter.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.constant: Operation time does not bound total entries.

items.prompt: A function returns a new array of k results and otherwise uses only two indices. Which classification is precise?

options.text: O(k) output space and O(1) auxiliary space.
O(k) auxiliary space and O(1) output space.
The same array counts as both output and auxiliary space.
Both are O(1) because only two indices are used.

feedback.reason: The result array is output storage, while the indices are working state.
feedback.details: Separating roles avoids conflating result size with auxiliary memory.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.aux_k: The new array is the required result.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.both_k: One structure should not be double-counted.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.all_constant: The result array grows with k.

items.prompt: A function sorts an input array in place using an implementation with O(log n) recursion depth. What auxiliary term must be included?

options.text: O(log n) recursion-stack space.
The input array itself as O(n) auxiliary space.
A mandatory new O(n) output array.
O(1) because the elements remain in the same array.

feedback.reason: In-place mutation can avoid a second array without eliminating call-stack storage.
feedback.details: Auxiliary analysis must include every simultaneously active frame.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.input_n_aux: Input storage is not extra working memory.

items.prompt: Why does the total number of allocations not by itself determine space complexity?

options.text: Allocations may be released or reused, so peak simultaneously live memory is what matters.
Allocations never consume memory.
Only variable names determine memory.
Allocation count is always identical to running time.

feedback.reason: Space complexity is not cumulative allocation traffic.
feedback.details: Lifetime overlap determines the maximum live footprint.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.time_equals_space: Time and peak space measure different resources.

items.prompt: Which storage categories should be separated in a careful analysis? Select all that apply.

options.text: Pre-existing input storage.
Storage required to represent the returned result.
Temporary working memory and call-stack state.
The number of source-code lines.

feedback.reason: Space explanations should identify storage by semantic role.
feedback.details: This prevents double-counting and makes in-place claims precise.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.source_lines: Runtime space does not scale with formatting.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.input: It may be reported but is not usually auxiliary.

items.prompt: Which structures contribute to auxiliary space in a boolean duplicate detector? Select all that apply.

options.text: A seen set discarded after returning the boolean.
A constant number of indices or iterators.
The returned boolean as O(1) output space.
The caller-provided input array.

feedback.reason: Temporary state is auxiliary even when it stores input-derived values.
feedback.details: The returned boolean and caller-owned input belong to different categories.

items.prompt: Which facts determine recursion-stack space? Select all that apply.

options.text: The maximum number of simultaneously active calls.
The asymptotic local storage retained by each frame.
The total number of calls over the whole execution.
The names of recursive functions.

feedback.reason: Peak recursive space is based on simultaneous frames, not cumulative call count.
feedback.details: Both depth and retained per-frame data matter.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.total_calls: Completed calls no longer occupy the stack.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.call_names: Names do not change frame size.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.frame_size: Large frame-local buffers can multiply with depth.

items.prompt: Which cases can have O(1) auxiliary space? Select all that apply.

options.text: An iterative in-place scan with a fixed number of scalars.
A streaming producer that emits results without retaining them and uses fixed local state.
A constant number of fixed-width masks.
A recursion of depth n with one frame per level.

feedback.reason: Auxiliary space depends on retained working state.
feedback.details: Large input or output does not automatically imply large auxiliary memory.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.stream_output: Logical output can be large while internal auxiliary state remains constant.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.fixed_word_mask: Their storage is constant under the stated model.

items.prompt: Arrange the process for deriving space complexity.

elements.text: List input, output, temporary structures, and recursion frames.
Determine the maximum simultaneously live size of each auxiliary component.
Add concurrent terms and simplify the dominant asymptotic bound.

feedback.reason: A space proof must account for both role and lifetime.
feedback.details: Adding every allocation ever made overstates peak space.

items.prompt: Arrange the analysis of recursive stack space.

elements.text: Multiply depth by asymptotic per-frame size when frames coexist.
Add other simultaneously live auxiliary structures.

feedback.reason: Recursive space follows concurrent frames and their retained state.
feedback.details: The final result may be dominated by either stack or another structure.

items.prompt: Arrange the comparison of two sequential temporary buffers.

elements.text: Determine the size of the first buffer.
Confirm it is released before the second buffer is allocated.
Use the maximum rather than the sum for peak auxiliary space.

feedback.reason: Non-overlapping lifetimes do not contribute simultaneously.
feedback.details: Peak live memory is the larger buffer plus any shared state.

items.prompt: An iterative scan uses three indices and returns no growing result. Select auxiliary-space complexity.
constraints: All scalar values have fixed size.

feedback.reason: A fixed number of fixed-size scalars does not grow with n.
feedback.details: Input size affects iteration count, not working storage.

items.prompt: A recursive function has depth n and stores an O(1) record in each active frame. Select auxiliary-space complexity.
constraints: No other growing structure is used.

feedback.reason: n constant-size frames coexist at maximum depth.
feedback.details: Total stack space is linear.

items.prompt: A divide-and-conquer recursion has depth O(log n), and every active frame stores O(1) local state. Select stack-space complexity.
constraints: Child calls are processed depth-first rather than retained simultaneously as full subtrees.

feedback.reason: Only one root-to-leaf chain is active in depth-first recursion.
feedback.details: Constant frame size times logarithmic depth gives logarithmic stack space.

items.prompt: A function builds an n by n boolean table and returns one scalar result. Select auxiliary-space and output-space complexity.
constraints: The table is discarded before return.

feedback.reason: The table is temporary working storage, while the result is constant-size.
feedback.details: The roles, not the data contents, determine classification.

items.prompt: A function returns k matched records in a new list and uses a hash set containing d temporary keys. Select auxiliary-space and output-space complexity.
constraints: The hash set is not returned.

feedback.reason: The temporary set and returned list have separate roles and parameters.
feedback.details: They may both be live near return, but auxiliary and output are reported separately.
